articles.title: Calcivena Ossification
articles.content:
[p]Progressive stiffening of joints and arteries as calcium deposits harden tissues. Victim’s movements become jerky and brittle.[/p]
[p]Speed reduced by 10 ft., disadvantage on Acrobatics checks.[/p]
[p]Chelation Nanodrones or Rascan bone-eating beetles (grim but effective).[/p]
[p]Total petrification within 3d6 days.[/p]

articles.title: Calcivena Ossification
articles.content:
[p]Progressive stiffening of joints and arteries as calcium deposits harden tissues. Victim’s movements become jerky and brittle.[/p]
[p]Speed reduced by 10 ft., disadvantage on Acrobatics checks.[/p]
[p]Chelation Nanodrones or Rascan bone-eating beetles (grim but effective).[/p]
[p]Total petrification within 3d6 days.[/p]

articles.title: Chirapsis Myelitis
articles.content:
[p]Tingling and weakness in the extremities, progressing to spasmodic hand movements (“chira” = hand) and partial paralysis. Eyes may show a faint phosphorescent halo under low light.[/p]
[p]-2 to Dexterity and disadvantage on skill checks requiring fine motor control (lockpicking, crafting).[/p]
[p]Synthesized Neurovenin Serum, extracted from Delvani brain coral symbiotes. Alternatively, a Delvani ritual to “untangle the nerves” (DC 18 Medicine or Arcana).[/p]
[p]Leads to full quadriplegia in 3d6 days if untreated.[/p]

articles.title: Chirapsis Myelitis
articles.content:
[p]Tingling and weakness in the extremities, progressing to spasmodic hand movements (“chira” = hand) and partial paralysis. Eyes may show a faint phosphorescent halo under low light.[/p]
[p]-2 to Dexterity and disadvantage on skill checks requiring fine motor control (lockpicking, crafting).[/p]
[p]Synthesized Neurovenin Serum, extracted from Delvani brain coral symbiotes. Alternatively, a Delvani ritual to “untangle the nerves” (DC 18 Medicine or Arcana).[/p]
[p]Leads to full quadriplegia in 3d6 days if untreated.[/p]

articles.title: Chronothorax Disorder
articles.content:
[p]Victim experiences time dilation: minutes feel like hours or hours like seconds. Speech slurring and limb delay ensues.[/p]
[p]Initiative is halved, and reactions are delayed (cannot take reactions).[/p]
[p]Administration of Temporal Stabilizers (found only in Vorraki black markets).[/p]
[p]Neural collapse occurs after 1d10 days.[/p]

articles.title: Chronothorax Disorder
articles.content:
[p]Victim experiences time dilation: minutes feel like hours or hours like seconds. Speech slurring and limb delay ensues.[/p]
[p]Initiative is halved, and reactions are delayed (cannot take reactions).[/p]
[p]Administration of Temporal Stabilizers (found only in Vorraki black markets).[/p]
[p]Neural collapse occurs after 1d10 days.[/p]

articles.title: Coriovex Dermal Syndrome
articles.content:
[p]Leathery skin plaques, extreme itchiness, and shedding of dermal layers. Advanced stages cause biofluorescence in cracked skin patterns.[/p]
[p]Disadvantage on Charisma checks (due to grotesque appearance) and -1 AC as dermal integrity weakens.[/p]
[p]Topical Synth-Steroids combined with Rascan fermented fungal poultices (requires a DC 15 Nature or Medicine check).[/p]
[p]Chronic if untreated, with potential for secondary infections (roll CON save every long rest or suffer 1d4 necrotic damage).[/p]

articles.title: Coriovex Dermal Syndrome
articles.content:
[p]Leathery skin plaques, extreme itchiness, and shedding of dermal layers. Advanced stages cause biofluorescence in cracked skin patterns.[/p]
[p]Disadvantage on Charisma checks (due to grotesque appearance) and -1 AC as dermal integrity weakens.[/p]
[p]Topical Synth-Steroids combined with Rascan fermented fungal poultices (requires a DC 15 Nature or Medicine check).[/p]
[p]Chronic if untreated, with potential for secondary infections (roll CON save every long rest or suffer 1d4 necrotic damage).[/p]

articles.title: Cryoviral Lesions
articles.content:
[p]Frostbite-like patches forming in warm temperatures; victims exhale chilled mist.[/p]
[p]Resistance to fire but vulnerability to bludgeoning (brittle tissues).[/p]
[p]Heated nanogel infusions or Felkin sunroot compresses.[/p]
[p]Body temp plummets to fatal hypothermia within 1d12 days.[/p]

articles.title: Cryoviral Lesions
articles.content:
[p]Frostbite-like patches forming in warm temperatures; victims exhale chilled mist.[/p]
[p]Resistance to fire but vulnerability to bludgeoning (brittle tissues).[/p]
[p]Heated nanogel infusions or Felkin sunroot compresses.[/p]
[p]Body temp plummets to fatal hypothermia within 1d12 days.[/p]

articles.title: Cryptothorax Myiasis
articles.content:
[p]Tiny, armored larvae burrow into the chest cavity, causing coughing fits and audible chittering sounds from within.[/p]
[p]-1 Constitution per day; violent coughing during stealth checks imposes disadvantage.[/p]
[p]Surgical extraction (DC 17 Medicine) or suffocating larvae with inhaled Pykarian Salt Smoke.[/p]
[p]Larvae mature in 7 days, erupting explosively (4d10 damage).[/p]

articles.title: Cryptothorax Myiasis
articles.content:
[p]Tiny, armored larvae burrow into the chest cavity, causing coughing fits and audible chittering sounds from within.[/p]
[p]-1 Constitution per day; violent coughing during stealth checks imposes disadvantage.[/p]
[p]Surgical extraction (DC 17 Medicine) or suffocating larvae with inhaled Pykarian Salt Smoke.[/p]
[p]Larvae mature in 7 days, erupting explosively (4d10 damage).[/p]

articles.title: Dreadmere Syndrome Radiation exposure from the Ashen Spiral
articles.content:
[p]Persistent nausea, grey pallor of the skin, hair loss, blackened fingernails. Advanced stages include coughing up soot-like particulates and phosphorescent lesions.[/p]
[p]•	-1 CON per long rest spent in the Spiral’s radiation.
	•	On failed DC 15 CON save, gain a level of Exhaustion.
	•	At Stage 3: Roll on a mutation table each long rest.[/p]
[p]Full-body cellular repair using Vorraki Helix Reweavers or a 2-week quarantine in Aestari biochambers.[/p]
[p]Fatal within 1d6 weeks untreated. Survivors may carry genetic anomalies.[/p]

articles.title: Embarrassalis Aestari blushing disorder
articles.content:
[p]Irregular patches of glowing blush on face, neck, and hands when nervous.[/p]
[p]Disadvantage on Deception checks.[/p]
[p]Meditation or Felkin herbal calming teas.[/p]
[p]Completely benign. May become a social meme.[/p]

articles.title: Feather Flu Mild mutation syndrome
articles.content:
[p]Tiny, soft feather-like growths appear randomly on skin after exposure to the Throne of Ambitions.[/p]
[p]No mechanical penalty, but 1d4 people per day comment awkwardly on your “new look.”[/p]
[p]have or pluck; they regrow in 1d4 days unless treated with melanin stabilizers.[/p]
[p]Benign; may amuse or horrify others.[/p]

articles.title: Flatulentus Minor Microbe imbalance
articles.content:
[p]Excessive gas production, amplified in zero-gravity environments.[/p]
[p]Any failed Stealth roll in confined spaces alerts nearby enemies with a faint pffft.[/p]
[p]Aestari probiotic capsules.[/p]
[p]Resolves naturally in 1 week.[/p]

articles.title: Fracture Pulse Syndrome Exposure to destabilized spaces
articles.content:
[p]Vertigo, nausea, perception of space as “folding and snapping.” Objects appear closer/farther unpredictably.[/p]
[p]•	Disadvantage on ranged attacks.
	•	25% chance per turn of moving 1d4 extra or fewer squares unintentionally.[/p]
[p]Exposure to stabilized space anchors (Delvani tech) or Rascan grounding runes.[/p]
[p]Neural degradation within 2d8 days; leads to spatial phobia and permanent -2 INT.[/p]

articles.title: Gastrothrix Ulceris
articles.content:
[p]Severe abdominal pain, vomiting dark bile, and visible writhing in the stomach under thin skin.[/p]
[p]Can’t take Dash or Disengage actions; long rests restore only half HP.[/p]
[p]or drinking Rascan firewort tea (DC 14 CON save to endure treatment).[/p]
[p]Organ rupture in 1d10 days, followed by sepsis.[/p]

articles.title: Gastrothrix Ulceris
articles.content:
[p]Severe abdominal pain, vomiting dark bile, and visible writhing in the stomach under thin skin.[/p]
[p]Can’t take Dash or Disengage actions; long rests restore only half HP.[/p]
[p]or drinking Rascan firewort tea (DC 14 CON save to endure treatment).[/p]
[p]Organ rupture in 1d10 days, followed by sepsis.[/p]

articles.title: Giggle Fits Risuvirus
articles.content:
[p]Uncontrollable giggling at inappropriate times, common in Rascan urban areas during spring.[/p]
[p]Any failed WIS save results in 1d4 rounds of incapacitation from laughter.[/p]
[p]Deep breathing, Felkin calming incense.[/p]
[p]Resolves in 2d6 hours.[/p]

articles.title: Helminthic Psychosis
articles.content:
[p]Victims complain of voices in their heads urging violent acts; post-mortem scans reveal wormlike entities in neural tissue.[/p]
[p]Roll WIS save each day. Failure causes erratic behavior; on critical failure, DM takes control of character for 1 hour.[/p]
[p]Exorcism by Rascan shamans or high-dose Antihelminthics with 50% success rate.[/p]
[p]Complete loss of autonomy in 1d6 days.[/p]

articles.title: Helminthic Psychosis
articles.content:
[p]Victims complain of voices in their heads urging violent acts; post-mortem scans reveal wormlike entities in neural tissue.[/p]
[p]Roll WIS save each day. Failure causes erratic behavior; on critical failure, DM takes control of character for 1 hour.[/p]
[p]Exorcism by Rascan shamans or high-dose Antihelminthics with 50% success rate.[/p]
[p]Complete loss of autonomy in 1d6 days.[/p]

articles.title: Hematovore Infestation
articles.content:
[p]Blood thickens into a sluggish, tar-like consistency as microscopic parasites feed on plasma proteins. Victim suffers fatigue and pale skin.[/p]
[p]Disadvantage on Strength-based rolls; -1 to maximum HP per long rest.[/p]
[p]High-frequency plasmapheresis or ingestion of Felkin venom sac extract.[/p]
[p]Fatal anemia in 1d8 days.[/p]

articles.title: Hematovore Infestation
articles.content:
[p]Blood thickens into a sluggish, tar-like consistency as microscopic parasites feed on plasma proteins. Victim suffers fatigue and pale skin.[/p]
[p]Disadvantage on Strength-based rolls; -1 to maximum HP per long rest.[/p]
[p]High-frequency plasmapheresis or ingestion of Felkin venom sac extract.[/p]
[p]Fatal anemia in 1d8 days.[/p]

articles.title: Lab Tan ATLAS Underground Syndrome
articles.content:
[p]Pale complexion with strange, uneven tanning from lab lights. Often leaves bizarre patterns resembling lab goggles or gloves.[/p]
[p]-1 Charisma for first impressions in sunlight cultures.[/p]
[p]A day in natural light or Delvani phototherapy lamps.[/p]
[p]Cosmetic only; harmless.[/p]

articles.title: Lithic Fever
articles.content:
[p]Skin hardens into brittle stone-like patches, fever spikes to 105°F, joints lock. Victims may shed flakes resembling rock dust.[/p]
[p]•	Movement speed halved.
	•	Disadvantage on Dexterity saves.[/p]
[p]Korrak geomantic bleeding rituals or Rascan heated mineral baths infused with pyrite dust.[/p]
[p]Complete petrification in 1d6 weeks.[/p]

articles.title: Lumenic Sickness Morbus Luminis
articles.content:
[p]Victim’s skin glows faintly in the dark; extreme photosensitivity develops causing burning pain in sunlight.[/p]
[p]Sunlight causes 1d8 radiant damage per round of exposure.[/p]
[p]Injection of Melanocrylate Serum followed by a 12-hour darkroom quarantine.[/p]
[p]ventually burns out retinal tissue and dermal cells; fatal in 1 month.[/p]

articles.title: Lumenic Sickness Morbus Luminis
articles.content:
[p]Victim’s skin glows faintly in the dark; extreme photosensitivity develops causing burning pain in sunlight.[/p]
[p]Sunlight causes 1d8 radiant damage per round of exposure.[/p]
[p]Injection of Melanocrylate Serum followed by a 12-hour darkroom quarantine.[/p]
[p]ventually burns out retinal tissue and dermal cells; fatal in 1 month.[/p]

articles.title: Luminic Psychosis Overexposure in the pull of light
articles.content:
[p]Euphoric mania, uncontrollable tears of liquid light, skin blistering under intense glow. Later, screaming episodes and light hallucinations.[/p]
[p]•	Must roll WIS save DC 12 hourly or act erratically (attacking allies, babbling incoherently).
	•	Suffer 1d6 radiant damage per hour.[/p]
[p]Full sensory deprivation in a Rascan “Null Room” or injection of Photonic Repressor Serum.[/p]
[p]Total neural burnout in 3d6 hours, leaving victim in permanent catatonia.[/p]

articles.title: Mycofloridiosis Carnal Bloom
articles.content:
[p]Victims experience an overwhelming floral scent emitted from their pores, coupled with pink fungal filaments sprouting from mucous membranes. Libido increases uncontrollably in early stages.[/p]
[p]•	Roll WIS save (DC 13) in intimate situations or lose self-control.
	•	-1 Constitution every long rest untreated.[/p]
[p]Aggressive antifungal therapy using Aestari Sporekillers or full-body UV irradiation (DC 15 CON save to survive treatment).[/p]
[p]Advanced stages result in parasitic mind control (NPC control during encounters) and death by systemic fungal colonization in 2d8 days.[/p]

articles.title: Necraphageic Fever Febris Necraphaga
articles.content:
[p]High fever, necrotic lesions at extremities, blackened veins visible beneath the skin.[/p]
[p]1 level of Exhaustion per day untreated. Attacks deal 1d6 necrotic damage to self on a critical failure.[/p]
[p]A transfusion using Vorraki “living blood reserves” or Bone Marrow Recalibration (DC 18 Medicine).[/p]
[p]Fatal within 2d4 days; survivors often lose fingers/toes to necrosis.[/p]

articles.title: Necraphageic Fever Febris Necraphaga
articles.content:
[p]High fever, necrotic lesions at extremities, blackened veins visible beneath the skin.[/p]
[p]1 level of Exhaustion per day untreated. Attacks deal 1d6 necrotic damage to self on a critical failure.[/p]
[p]A transfusion using Vorraki “living blood reserves” or Bone Marrow Recalibration (DC 18 Medicine).[/p]
[p]Fatal within 2d4 days; survivors often lose fingers/toes to necrosis.[/p]

articles.title: Neurochoric Dissonance
articles.content:
[p]Conflicting auditory hallucinations, inability to focus on single conversations, frequent migraines.[/p]
[p]Disadvantage on Initiative and all skill checks involving communication.[/p]
[p]Vorraki neural sync implant recalibration or Felkin “silence circle” ritual.[/p]
[p]Catatonia in 2d4 weeks.[/p]

articles.title: Neurochoric Dissonance
articles.content:
[p]Conflicting auditory hallucinations, inability to focus on single conversations, frequent migraines.[/p]
[p]Disadvantage on Initiative and all skill checks involving communication.[/p]
[p]Vorraki neural sync implant recalibration or Felkin “silence circle” ritual.[/p]
[p]Catatonia in 2d4 weeks.[/p]

articles.title: Neurolux Collapse
articles.content:
[p]Affects neural bio-lumens in Aestari and humans with neural implants, causing memory fragmentation and sporadic seizures.[/p]
[p]Roll d20 on spellcasting—on a 1–2, spell fails with neural backlash (1d6 psychic damage).[/p]
[p]Neural firmware re-flash by Vorraki technicians or Mindlace Resonance Therapy.[/p]
[p]Complete neural shutdown in 3 weeks.[/p]

articles.title: Neurolux Collapse
articles.content:
[p]Affects neural bio-lumens in Aestari and humans with neural implants, causing memory fragmentation and sporadic seizures.[/p]
[p]Roll d20 on spellcasting—on a 1–2, spell fails with neural backlash (1d6 psychic damage).[/p]
[p]Neural firmware re-flash by Vorraki technicians or Mindlace Resonance Therapy.[/p]
[p]Complete neural shutdown in 3 weeks.[/p]

articles.title: Oculus Hemorrhagicus
articles.content:
[p]Eyes redden with capillary rupture, vision blurs into crimson hues. Occasional spontaneous bleeding from tear ducts.[/p]
[p]Disadvantage on ranged attacks and Perception checks relying on sight.[/p]
[p]Oculostatic Nanogel Drops (delicate application, DC 14 Medicine) or a Felkin herbal decoction brewed under a full moon.[/p]
[p]Permanent blindness in 1 week untreated.[/p]

articles.title: Oculus Hemorrhagicus
articles.content:
[p]Eyes redden with capillary rupture, vision blurs into crimson hues. Occasional spontaneous bleeding from tear ducts.[/p]
[p]Disadvantage on ranged attacks and Perception checks relying on sight.[/p]
[p]Oculostatic Nanogel Drops (delicate application, DC 14 Medicine) or a Felkin herbal decoction brewed under a full moon.[/p]
[p]Permanent blindness in 1 week untreated.[/p]

articles.title: Phantom Glow
articles.content:
[p]A faint flickering halo around the head at night, caused by anomaly energy residue.[/p]
[p]Advantage on Intimidation (spooky), disadvantage on Stealth.[/p]
[p]Exposure to grounding fields or Rascan shadow baths.[/p]
[p]Fades over 1 week.[/p]

articles.title: Photomorphosis Syndrome
articles.content:
[p]Exposure to light causes victims’ skin to sprout photosynthetic fronds, while eyes grow reflective membranes.[/p]
[p]Regains 1 HP per hour in sunlight, but radiant damage vulnerability x2.[/p]
[p]Full-body dermabrasion under moonlight and injection of Melanocyte Repressors.[/p]
[p]Eventually fuses victim to plant-like biomass.[/p]

articles.title: Photomorphosis Syndrome
articles.content:
[p]Exposure to light causes victims’ skin to sprout photosynthetic fronds, while eyes grow reflective membranes.[/p]
[p]Regains 1 HP per hour in sunlight, but radiant damage vulnerability x2.[/p]
[p]Full-body dermabrasion under moonlight and injection of Melanocyte Repressors.[/p]
[p]Eventually fuses victim to plant-like biomass.[/p]

articles.title: Plasmocystis Intima Slick Rot
articles.content:
[p]Sticky, mucosal discharge with a faint iridescence; tissue irritation leads to ulcers on genitalia and inner thighs. Infected individuals emit a faint sweet scent.[/p]
[p]•	Disadvantage on Athletics and Acrobatics checks (due to discomfort).
	•	Risk of partner infection on failed CON save (DC 14).[/p]
[p]Localized application of Biofilm Dissolvers followed by systemic Antiparasitic Regimen.[/p]
[p]Progresses to tissue necrosis and sepsis in 1d8 weeks without intervention.[/p]

articles.title: Pulvinar Atrophy
articles.content:
[p]Muscular wasting, especially in shoulders and thighs; victims complain of “hollowness” in their limbs.[/p]
[p]-1 Strength per long rest until treated.[/p]
[p]A diet rich in Aestari protein suspensions and application of kinesio-regenerative therapies.[/p]
[p]Victim becomes bedridden within 5 days. Recovery takes 2 weeks even after treatment.[/p]

articles.title: Pulvinar Atrophy
articles.content:
[p]Muscular wasting, especially in shoulders and thighs; victims complain of “hollowness” in their limbs.[/p]
[p]-1 Strength per long rest until treated.[/p]
[p]A diet rich in Aestari protein suspensions and application of kinesio-regenerative therapies.[/p]
[p]Victim becomes bedridden within 5 days. Recovery takes 2 weeks even after treatment.[/p]

articles.title: Pyrovex Dermatitis
articles.content:
[p]Victim’s sweat becomes flammable, skin emits faint heat waves.[/p]
[p]Physical exertion has 10% chance per round of igniting sweat (1d8 fire damage).[/p]
[p]Cryo-vasodilators or Rascan ice-bath ritual.[/p]
[p]Total epidermal combustion after 2 weeks untreated.[/p]

articles.title: Pyrovex Dermatitis
articles.content:
[p]Victim’s sweat becomes flammable, skin emits faint heat waves.[/p]
[p]Physical exertion has 10% chance per round of igniting sweat (1d8 fire damage).[/p]
[p]Cryo-vasodilators or Rascan ice-bath ritual.[/p]
[p]Total epidermal combustion after 2 weeks untreated.[/p]

articles.title: Rascan Itchfoot Pedis Rascanis
articles.content:
[p]Mild itching and tingling between toes, worsens after exposure to Rascan fungal carpets.[/p]
[p]Minor distraction—DC 10 CON save to avoid disadvantage on stealth rolls.[/p]
[p]Over-the-counter antifungal powder or a Rascan “foot cleanse” ritual involving fermented hops.[/p]
[p]Clears in 1d4 days with treatment; harmless otherwise.[/p]

articles.title: Rascani Worms Parasitus Rascaniensis
articles.content:
[p]Subcutaneous wriggling sensation, visible under thin skin. Occasional vomiting of small white worms.[/p]
[p]Disadvantage on Constitution saving throws. Cannot benefit from short rests unless treated.[/p]
[p]Consumption of Pykarian Bitterroot (induces violent expulsion) or high-frequency sound bath (DC 16 Arcana).[/p]
[p]Worms reach the brain in 2d6 days, turning the host into a mindless husk (NPC control).[/p]

articles.title: Rascani Worms Parasitus Rascaniensis
articles.content:
[p]Subcutaneous wriggling sensation, visible under thin skin. Occasional vomiting of small white worms.[/p]
[p]Disadvantage on Constitution saving throws. Cannot benefit from short rests unless treated.[/p]
[p]Consumption of Pykarian Bitterroot (induces violent expulsion) or high-frequency sound bath (DC 16 Arcana).[/p]
[p]Worms reach the brain in 2d6 days, turning the host into a mindless husk (NPC control).[/p]

articles.title: Saelirion Vaelthas
articles.credits:
[h|e15e56d0-4445-4f3d-9a5f-ec369c62123f][b]Keeper of Ash and Memory[/b][/h]
[p]Saelirion Vaelthas was born beneath pale canopies where light filters as thought rather than sun, in one of the elder Aestari enclaves that remembers Everbright not as a place, but as a direction. He has outlived four mortal civilizations and witnessed two philosophies of history rise, ossify, and collapse under their own certainty.[/p]
[p]He is not a priest, nor a prophet. He names himself a keeper, not of truth, but of accounts. Saelirion holds that history is never clean, never singular, and never safe. What survives is not what happened, but what endured being remembered.[/p]
[p]In his later centuries, Saelirion withdrew from courts of Light and from academies devoted to perfect form. He took residence near the Veil, where clarity frays, where memory learns humility, and where contradictions feel honest. There, he gathered testimonies, half-dreams, forbidden fragments, and records that could not agree with themselves.[/p]
[p]What follows is his account. It is not doctrine. It is not reassurance. It is offered as warning.[/p]
[p][br][/p]
[p][/p]
